input:
I said: “I am an Arab and I have heard that you are mobilizing a force to attack that man and have decided to join you.” He said: “I am doing that indeed.” I walked with him a little until I could take him unawares. Then, at the right moment, I hit him with my sword and killed him. I left the place, with his women crying over his body. When I arrived in Madinah the Prophet saw me and said: “Successful is the mission.” I said: “I have killed him, Messenger of God.” He said: “That is right.” Playing a Dirty Trick The Hudhayl were enraged at the assassination of their leader.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 22: Vulnerability and Treachery

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 22: Vulnerability and Treachery

characters.name: Umar ibn Al-Khattab
characters.appearance: Late 20s, dark olive skin, athletic build. Short dark beard, neat. Wearing a brown thobe, plain leather belt, worn leather sandals. Strong facial features, sharp eyes. Distinguished by athletic build and sharp facial features.

characters.name: The Leader of the Hudhayl Tribe
characters.appearance: Late 30s, dark olive skin, medium build. Wearing a simple brown thobe, no head covering. Strong facial features, stern expression. Distinguished by strong facial features and stern expression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 30s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: Honesty and truth are always better than deceit and trickery.
output.narrative_text: Umar tricked the leader of the Hudhayl tribe into coming out, then killed him. When he returned to Madinah, the Prophet was pleased with his success. But was Umar's trick honest and fair?

compliance.notes: Prophet not shown in the scene, but mentioned. Umar's actions are depicted in a neutral way to avoid glorifying violence. Appropriate for children, with a focus on the moral lesson.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Desert landscape with rocky outcrops and palm trees. Umar, a young Arab man with dark olive skin, athletic build, and short dark beard, seen from the side, holding a sword and standing over the body of the Hudhayl leader, a middle-aged Arab man seen from behind, wearing a simple brown thobe. The Prophet is shown from behind, seen in the distance, walking towards Umar. Warm golden palette, with the sun shining down. No modern objects.
